analysis.overall_assessment: The single detected tea leaf shows severe discoloration and necrotic lesions, indicating complete loss of marketable quality. No healthy tissue remains for premium processing.

pollution_reduction_solutions: Install ozone-absorbing buffer zones with species like silver birch or poplar around tea fields
Apply kaolin-based particle film sprays to create protective leaf barriers
Implement real-time air quality monitoring with automatic irrigation activation during high-ozone periods

immediate_actions: Isolate and quarantine all harvested leaves from affected sections
Conduct laboratory analysis to confirm pollutant concentration levels
Suspend processing of current batch until root cause is identified
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: Complete rejection of all leaves from affected plants; no salvageable material exists
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Not applicable - leaves must be diverted from commercial processing
quality_preservation: Document affected areas for future reference
Preserve leaf samples for pollutant analysis

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: No marketable portions remain; entire leaf is compromised
alternative_uses: Soil amendment for ornamental plants (after pollutant testing)
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Shred leaves and mix with 3:1 ratio of carbon-rich materials (sawdust, straw); maintain 55-60°C for 14 days to break down pollutants
prevention_measures: Establish 50-meter buffer zones with pollution-tolerant vegetation
Implement predictive air quality alerts for harvest scheduling

cost_saving_opportunities: Redirect rejected leaves to low-cost composting operations
Claim insurance coverage for pollution-related crop loss
Negotiate with local farms for compost exchange programs
value_recovery_methods: Convert to organic fertilizer for non-food crops
Use in biogas production if facilities available

priority_actions: Implement immediate ozone monitoring system with SMS alerts
Plant 30% of field perimeter with ozone-absorbing species within 30 days
Conduct soil and leaf tissue analysis to determine residual pollutant levels
recommendations.timeline: Critical actions within 7 days; full remediation plan execution within 90 days
